message:
refactor(modal): extract ModalFocusCoordinator and rebuild focus trap lifecycle

The modal focus integration layer was growing increasingly tangled —
push, pop, pop-by-id, pop-all, and event-dispatch each reimplemented
subtly different focus-group teardown logic, leading to edge cases where
focus traps leaked or base-focus restoration was skipped.

This refactor extracts a ModalFocusCoordinator struct that encapsulates
the focus-manager ↔ modal-stack handshake:
- push_modal_with_trap: allocates a focus group, pushes a trap, and
  records base_focus only on the first trap (not on nested modals)
- pop_modal / pop_modal_by_id / pop_all_modals: each correctly tears
  down focus groups and rebuilds the trap stack via rebuild_focus_traps
- handle_modal_event: dispatches events and processes any resulting
  modal close, including focus cleanup
- rebuild_focus_traps: replays the full trap stack from scratch when
  out-of-order pops occur, and restores base_focus when the stack
  empties (handling both Some(focus) and None/blurred states)

FocusAwareModalStack now delegates to the coordinator, which eliminates
~200 lines of duplicated teardown logic. base_focus is now
Option<Option<FocusId>> to correctly distinguish "no modals have been
opened" from "focus was None/blurred when the first modal opened."

Additional fixes:
- FocusManager gains repair_focus_after_excluding_ids for safe post-
  group-removal focus fixup, and focus_first_without_history_for_restore
- Frame hit-testing consolidated into HitTestResult (replacing the prior
  HitData/HitId/HitRegion triple import)
- Help widget updated for the simplified hit-test API
- All 7 modal snapshot tests updated to reflect the new rendering

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
